question.text: Under Rule 9(1A) of the PML Rules, a reporting entity must file electronic copies of client KYC records with the Central KYC Records Registry (CKYCR) within __________ after commencement of an account-based relationship.

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Rule 9(1A) mandates that every reporting entity shall, within 10 days of commencement of an account-based relationship with a client, file the electronic copy of the client's KYC records with the Central KYC Records Registry (CKYCR). This ensures centralized storage and accessibility of KYC data across financial institutions.
**The Trap:** 5 days is too short and not specified. 15 and 30 days are common distractor periods from other compliance rules. The specific timeline under Rule 9(1A) is exactly 10 days — this is a frequently tested numerical fact.
**Cross-Exam Context:** CKYCR was established to centralize KYC records and avoid duplication of KYC processes across different financial institutions. Once KYC is recorded on CKYCR, other reporting entities can retrieve it using the KYC Identification Number (KIN).
**Key Takeaway:** 10 days is the deadline for filing KYC with CKYCR after account opening. Remember this number — Rule 9(1A) = 10 days to CKYCR.
